channel.title: GAME Guide - No Fluff, Just Fixes on Crime Empire Guide
channel.description: Recent content in GAME Guide - No Fluff, Just Fixes on Crime Empire Guide

item.title: DDS2 Meth Recipe: Cook Your First Batch (Step-by-Step)
item.description:
The DDS2 Datamine v2 (drugdealersim.com) catalogs 73 substances. Meth starts at POT 0.88 (pure). The first cook is straightforward once you know the ingredient chain: ephedrine -> compound -> purify -> cut.

Step 1: Source the Ingredients

Based on the DDS2 Datamine v2 (verified via drugdealersim.com wiki and Steam FAQ guide):

Icon Ingredient Source Toxicity
Dds2 Ephedrine (EPHE) Pharmacy 0.1 (low)
Iodoric Acid (HI) Chemical supplier 0.6 (medium)
Methylamine (METHYL) Farm supply 0.6 (medium)
Ammonia (AMMONIA) Hardware store 0.4 (medium)
Water Any vendor 0.0 (none)
Do NOT use gasoline or methanol as substitutes. The Datamine confirms TOX: 0.9 for both -- you will poison your customer base rapidly.

Step 2: Create the Compound

The Datamine lists METH-COMP (Methamphetamine Compound) as:

item.title: DDS2 Achievement Guide: Full List (39 Achievements Verified)
item.description:
Drug Dealer Simulator 2 has 39 Steam achievements (confirmed on the official Steam page). Achievement tracking sites like TrueAchievements and Steam Hunters document the full list. Here is the verified guide to unlocking them.

All Achievements by Category

Based on data from Steam Hunters and PlayTracker achievement tracking sites.

Story and Progression Achievements:

Achievement Unlock Condition Unlock Rate (Steam)
I Am The One Who Knocks Achieve Street Cred level 4 Common
Higher learning Progress through story Common
The Kingpin Reach max Street Cred Rare
Side hustle Complete side missions ~0.6% (rare)

Collectible and Misc Achievements:

item.title: DDS2 Staff and Employee Guide: How to Hire and Assign
item.description:
Employees in DDS2 are essential for scaling past the early game. The Steam FAQ guide covers "how to get more employees, from early-game strategies to unlocking advanced methods." You can hire through notice boards, influencer missions, and story progression.

How to Get Employees

Verified methods from Steam community discussions:

Method How Limit
Notice boards Random spawns at hideout locations No known limit
Influencer missions Complete tasks for named NPCs 2 hires per influencer
Story progression Unlock through main questline Story-gated
Territory expansion Some hideouts include staff unlocks Limited
Steam discussions confirm you can ask influencers for workers twice each. After that, notice boards and missions are the primary sources.

Employee Roles

Based on YouTube guide descriptions and the Steam FAQ:

item.title: DDS2 Parkour Guide: Movement Mechanics and Police Escape
item.description:
The DDS2 Fandom tutorial page confirms: "Use parkour to find shortcuts, to make it easier to escape from enemies chasing you, or to sneak into otherwise inaccessible areas." Parkour is your primary survival tool -- not guns.

Parkour Actions

Action Key Use Case
Vault Space (toward obstacle) Low fences, barriers
Wall Run Space (toward wall at angle) Narrow alleys, building gaps
Jump Climb Space (toward ledge) Roof access, vertical escape
Sprint Shift Open ground between cover
Crouch/Slide Ctrl Break line of sight

The Fandom wiki confirms these moves are specifically designed for:

item.title: DDS2 Hideout Locations Guide and Raid Protection
item.description:
Hideouts in DDS2 can be raided based on your wanted level and the hideout's visibility. The community guide "Pedro Alvarez Real Estate Agency" documents all locations with prices, water/phone line availability, and visibility ratings. Here is what you need to know.

How Raids Work

The DDS2 Fandom hideout page confirms:

  • Raid probability is affected by wanted level (None / Person of Interest / Suspect / Wanted)
  • Hideout visibility is a factor -- highly visible locations attract more attention
  • Helicopters can appear during raids
  • A single hideout with all your assets is a high-risk strategy
Wanted Level Raid Probability Protection Strategy
None Low Standard precautions
Person of Interest Medium Split assets, 2+ hideouts
Suspect High Guards, hidden compartments
Wanted Very High Minimize time at any hideout

Verified Hideout Purchase Order

Based on cross-referencing the community hideout guide and Reddit discussions:

item.title: Best Early-Game Money Methods in DDS2 (Verified)
item.description:
The fastest early-game money in DDS2 comes from delivery grinding and territory expansion, not cooking. Verified through Steam community quick-start strategy guide and Reddit player discussions: in the first 10 hours, deliveries outperform cooking by roughly 3:1.

Phase 1: Delivery Economy ($0 to $2,000)

Your first $2,000 should come from ShadyComm deliveries. Per the Steam quick-start guide: "Police hours bring extra cash, so try to keep moving those drugs. The faster you deliver, the faster they re-order."

item.title: DDS2 Beginner's Guide: From Beach to First $5K
item.description:
Short answer: Your first hour is about survival, not profits. Complete the tutorial missions, meet your contact Shady, secure your first hideout, and make deliveries until you hit $5,000. Cooking comes after you have a reliable client pipeline.

Your First Steps on Isla Sombra

You wash up on a beach with nothing. No money, no contacts. Isla Sombra is a Caribbean dictatorship where power is the only law. Here is the sequence the game's economy is balanced around.

item.title: Crime Simulator 4-Player Co-op Strategy Guide
item.description:
In 4-player co-op, the fastest setup is: 1 Lockpick (entry), 1 Lookout (perimeter), 1 Hauler (loot collection), 1 Driver (escape). Rotate roles every 2-3 runs for flexibility.

Why Co-op Changes Everything

Crime Simulator supports up to 4 players in co-op. While solo play is viable, co-op doubles payout potential and dramatically reduces risk. More players mean more eyes on security, faster looting, and bigger scores.

But without coordination, co-op can be chaotic and counterproductive.

item.title: Crime Simulator Achievement Guide Part 1: Easy to Medium Achievements
item.description:
Crime Simulator has 60 Steam achievements total. About half of them come naturally through normal gameplay. This guide covers the 30 easiest achievements you can get in your first 10 hours.

How Achievement Hunting Works

Crime Simulator tracks achievements across all save files and game modes. You do not need 100% achievements in a single run. Use Completionist mode for hunting -- it removes quota pressure and lets you take all the time you need.

item.title: Crime Simulator Fast Money Guide: Best Early-Game Methods
item.description:
The fastest first $1,000 in Crime Simulator: complete the tutorial > pick up the crowbar > rob the nearest low-security house > sell electronics at the fence > buy a lockpick set. Repeat with better entry on house #2. You will clear $1,000 in about 20 minutes.

Early Game Money Loop (First Hour)

The opening hours are about capital accumulation. Every dollar goes back into tools that enable better heists.

Step 1: Tutorial Completion

The tutorial gives you starter cash and the crowbar. Do not skip it even if you have played similar games. The tutorial introduces Crime Simulator's specific lockpicking and stealth mechanics.

item.title: Crime Simulator Hardcore Mode Survival Guide
item.description:
Hardcore mode deletes your save on arrest. If you are reading this guide before your first Hardcore run, good. Read it twice. One mistake and you start from zero.

Hardcore vs Standard: What Changes

Mechanic Standard Hardcore
Permadeath No Yes
Quota scaling Normal Higher (about 1.5x)
Tool carry-over Yes on death No on run end
Skill retention Yes Per run only
Police aggression Normal Increased
Fence prices Normal About 20% lower

The core gameplay loop is the same, but the margin for error is razor thin.

item.title: Crime Simulator Loot Locations and Item Values Guide
item.description:
Electronics and jewelry give the best weight-to-value ratio. A laptop is worth roughly 5x what a kitchen appliance sells for and takes the same inventory slot.

Loot Tiers by Value

Not all loot is created equal. Prioritize these items when you see them:

High Value ($200+ each)

  • Laptops and tablets
  • Jewelry (rings, watches, necklaces)
  • Gold bars (Update 1.3)
  • Cash stacks
  • Smartphones

Medium Value ($80-$200)

  • Gaming consoles
  • Power tools
  • Brand-name clothing
  • Cameras
  • Stereos

Low Value (under $80)

  • Kitchen appliances
  • Decorations
  • Books
  • Clothing
  • General household items
Always pick up cash stacks first. They take no extra inventory management -- cash auto-converts to currency when you leave. Free money.

House Types and Expected Loot

Different house layouts favor different loot types:

item.title: Crime Simulator Tools Guide: Every Item Explained
item.description:
Prioritize the lockpick set first. It replaces the noisy crowbar on most locked doors and is the single best early-game investment.

Tool Overview

Crime Simulator has a growing arsenal of tools for breaking in, disabling security, and escaping. Tools break with use and must be repaired or replaced at the shop. Choosing the right tool for each job is the difference between a clean score and an arrest.

Lockpick Set

The bread and butter of every heist. Lockpicks let you open locked doors silently. Without them, you rely on the crowbar, which is loud and alerts nearby NPCs.

item.title: Getting Started in Crime Simulator
item.description:
Stuck in the tutorial? Follow this route: Complete the tutorial objective > pick up the crowbar > rob your first house > sell the loot at the fence > buy better tools before your next run.

First 15 Minutes: What Matters

You start Crime Simulator in debt. The game throws you into a tutorial that teaches the core loop: find a house, break in, steal valuables, sell them, repeat. The tutorial is short but skipping its lessons on lockpicking and stealth will cost you later.

item.title: Best Drug Recipe Profit Rankings
item.description:
Short answer: OG Kush 3-mix with Horse semen + Mega bean + Battery gives $136 profit per unit at rank Peddler III 鈥?best profit-per-effort before automation. If you have Chemists running and want max per-unit profit, build the Cocaine 9-mix at Kingpin: $717 net per unit.

The Upgrade Path in 5 Steps

From Hoodlum to Kingpin, your recipe choices determine how fast you scale. Here is the exact sequence.

1
Hoodlum Tier 1
Og kush + Banana + Cuke
Cost ~$6 鈫?/span> Sell ~$40 路 Profit ~$34

Buy everything from Albert and the first gas station 鈥?no alternative at this rank.

item.title: DDS2 Cutting Agents Guide: Maximize Profit Without Killing Customers
item.description:
The DDS2 Datamine v2 reveals that cutting agents have three key properties: TOX (toxicity), POT (potency multiplier), and ADD (addiction). Use low-TOX agents like flour and sugar to double your volume without losing customers.

How Cutting Agents Work

From the Datamine: each cutting agent modifies the final product's stats. The goal is to maximize volume while keeping TOX low enough that customers survive to re-order.

Property Meaning Optimal Value
TOX (Toxicity) Customer health risk As low as possible (under 0.5)
POT (Potency) Multiplies drug strength Over 1.0 = stronger
ADD (Addiction) Customer dependency Low keeps customers alive longer
STR (Strength) Perceived quality Over 1.0 = customers happy

Top Cutting Agents Ranked

Rank Agent TOX POT ADD STR Best Use
1 Flour 0.0 - - - Safe volume increase (baking base)
2 Salt 0.0 - - - Completely safe, no side effects
3 Dds2 sugar Sugar 0.0 - - - Safe, widely available
4 Water 0.0 - - - Base diluent, no effect
5 Vinegar 0.0 - - - Safe but limited use
6 Soda (baking) 0.1 - - - Minimal risk
7 Dds2 Ephedrine 0.1 - - - Low toxicity source
8 Cough Syrup 0.0 1.01 0.1 - Boosts potency slightly
9 Migraine Meds 0.0 1.01 0.1 - Boosts potency slightly
10 Ethanol 0.2 - 0.1 1.05 Risk-reward tradeoff
NEVER USE: Gasoline (TOX: 0.9), Methanol (TOX: 0.9), Washing Powder (TOX: 0.7), Explosive Compound (TOX: 0.9). These will rapidly kill your customer base.

Advanced Cutting: Intermediate Compounds

The Datamine lists several intermediate compounds that can be used as cutting bases:

item.title: DDS2 Hidden and Secret Achievements (Verified via Steam Trackers)
item.description:
DDS2 has several achievements with hidden unlock conditions. By cross-referencing Steam community guides (including the "EN Achievements 100%" guide) and tracking sites, we can piece together the requirements for the hardest-to-unlock achievements.

Achievements with Unknown Conditions

Several achievements on Steam Hunters and PlayTracker have descriptions that do not clearly state their unlock conditions:

Achievement Possible Trigger (Community Guesses) Rarity
Never let you down Complete a specific quest chain Very rare
A warm place Related to hideout upgrades or specific location Very rare
Home sweet home Fully upgrade a hideout Rare
Side hustle Complete all available side missions 0.6%
500 CIGARETTES Sell 500 cigarettes total 0.7%

DLC Achievements

The Casino DLC (released June 2025) adds additional achievements. The Steam community 100% guide notes that one involves killing the casino boss with a bomb and paying B4,000,000 afterward.

item.title: DDS2 Employee Role Assignments: Optimal Staff Setup
item.description:
Not all staff roles are equally important at every stage. The optimal hire order, verified through community guides, is: Dealer (first for automated income) -> Cook (scales production) -> Guard (protects investment) -> Distributor (enables multi-hideout logistics).

Role Breakdown

Dealers handle customer interactions. The DDS2 Fandom wiki explains that once you hire a dealer and set them up in any territory, you can assign customers from that region to them. This is the first automation unlock and should be your priority.

item.title: DDS2 Police Evasion: Parkour Escape Routes and Heat Management
item.description:
Police in DDS2 have a heat system similar to open-world crime games. Your wanted level (None -> Person of Interest -> Suspect -> Wanted) determines pursuit intensity. Parkour breaks line of sight faster than any other method.

Wanted Level System

Based on the DDS2 Fandom hideout page and community discussions:

Level Name Police Behavior How to Reduce
0 None Standard patrols No action needed
1 Person of Interest Increased patrols in area 3-5 min inactivity
2 Suspect Active search, roadblocks 5-10 min, avoid crime
3 Wanted Pursuit, helicopter 10+ min, hide in safe spot

Parkour Escape Flow

When police start chasing:

item.title: DDS2 Raid Prevention: Protecting Your Assets
item.description:
Getting raided in DDS2 is preventable. The game's raid system checks wanted level and hideout visibility. Keep both low and your assets will stay safe. Here is the verified strategy from community guides.

Raid Cause and Effect

Factor Impact on Raid Chance How to Control
Wanted level Direct multiplier Avoid illegal activity in populated areas
Hideout visibility Location-dependent Choose rural/jungle hideouts for labs
Product volume Indirect (attracts attention) Split across locations
Guard presence Reduces probability Hire guards when available

Asset Protection Strategy

Three-hideout model (community standard):

item.title: DDS2 Late-Game Empire: Scaling with Employees and Multi-Hideout Strategy
item.description:
Scaling past $50,000 requires employees, multiple specialized hideouts, and territory control. The DDS2 community on Reddit organizes by product type: one hideout for cannabis Dds2 indica , one for amphetamine Dds2 , one for meth Dds2 meth . Here is the verified path to a million-dollar operation.

Employee System

Employees are unlocked through:

  • Notice boards (random spawn)
  • Influencer missions (limited uses per influencer)
  • Story progression

The Steam FAQ guide covers "how to get more employees, from early-game strategies to unlocking advanced methods." Once hired, employees can be assigned to:

item.title: 10 DDS2 Tips the Game Doesn't Tell You (Serper Verified)
item.description:
The DDS2 tutorial covers basic controls but skips the survival mechanics that experienced players rely on. These 10 tips come from cross-referencing Steam community guides, Reddit discussions, and TheGamer's beginner guide -- all verified through Google search.

Survival Tips

1
Use Multiple Hideouts Early

Hideout raid risk is affected by wanted level and visibility. The DDS2 Fandom wiki confirms that during a raid, helicopters may appear and you can lose stored assets. Community strategy: split raw materials, finished product, and cash across 3+ hideouts. Never keep everything in one location.

item.title: Crime Simulator Achievement Guide Part 2: Hard Achievements
item.description:
Some of these achievements require multiple playthroughs or very specific conditions. Do not attempt them until you have completed the game in Standard mode and understand all mechanics.

Challenge Achievements (15)

Challenges are optional objectives that appear during runs. Completing them in specific quantities unlocks these achievements.

Key "Challenges" Achievements

Achievement Requirement Difficulty
Insane victory Complete 5 challenges in one game Medium
Master challenger Complete 10 challenges in one game Hard
Challenge addict Complete 25 challenges total Medium
Speed demon Complete 3 time-based challenges Hard
Silent but deadly Complete 3 stealth challenges Medium
Heavy hitter Complete 3 destruction challenges Medium
All-rounder Complete one of each challenge type Very Hard

Challenge Types and How to Trigger

Challenge Type Trigger Condition Example
Time-based Enter a house within a time limit "Steal 3 items within 30 seconds"
Stealth No detections during heist "Complete house without being seen"
Destruction Use force to enter "Smash 3 windows in one heist"
Collection Gather specific items "Collect 5 electronics"
Tool mastery Use specific tool set "Open all doors with lockpicks"
For "All-rounder," use a large house with multiple approaches. The ground floor can be stealth, basement can be destruction, and upstairs can be time-based. One house can satisfy all challenge types.

Speed Achievements (5)

These have strict time requirements.

item.title: Crime Simulator Co-op Heist Planning: From Setup to Extraction
item.description:
A clean heist is planned before you leave the hideout. The best teams spend 5 minutes planning and 10 minutes executing. Spend those 5 minutes -- they save you from 20 minutes of prison time.

Heist Planning Checklist

Before leaving the hideout, confirm:

  • [ ] Target selected from job board or free-roam
  • [ ] Security level assessed (locks, NPCs, alarms)
  • [ ] Required tools confirmed for each team member
  • [ ] Entry point identified (front, back, roof, window)
  • [ ] Extraction point agreed (vehicle, alley, rooftop)
  • [ ] Role assignments confirmed
  • [ ] Hard abort condition set (e.g., 2 police alerts = retreat)

Target Selection

Not all houses are equal. Evaluate each target based on:

item.title: Crime Simulator Game Modes and Quota System Explained
item.description:
If you keep failing quotas: focus on high-value items (electronics, jewelry, gold bars) instead of quantity. One big score beats ten small hauls for quota progress.

Game Modes Overview

Crime Simulator offers three distinct modes, each changing how the game plays and how you progress:

Mode Difficulty Permadeath Quota Best For
Standard Normal No Yes Learning mechanics
Hardcore Hard Yes Yes Experienced players
Completionist Variable No No Achievement hunters

Standard Mode

The default experience. You respawn at your hideout if arrested, keeping your tools and skill progress but losing current loot and a cash penalty. The quota system is active: you must earn a minimum amount by the end of each day or face consequences.

item.title: Crime Simulator Gold Bars and Jewelry Economy (Update 1.3)
item.description:
Do not sell gold bars for quick cash. Gold bars are the currency for hideout basement upgrades in Update 1.3 -- and those upgrades unlock higher-value contracts that pay back 5x the gold's fence price.

What Changed in 1.3

Update 1.3 (released December 2025) introduced a parallel economy system on top of the standard cash flow:

  • Gold bars -- a new resource for hideout basement upgrades
  • Jewelry loot -- authentic and fake variants
  • Basement hideout expansion -- upgradeable with gold bars
  • PvP competition modes -- new risk-reward income
  • Master Key -- premium tool for instant lock opening

Gold Bars: The Real Progression

Gold bars are found in high-security targets, heist locations, and rare loot boxes. Unlike cash, gold bars are heavy and take multiple inventory slots.

item.title: Crime Simulator Hardcore Mode Progression: From Zero to Rank 6+
item.description:
This guide assumes you have read the Hardcore survival basics. If you have not completed a single Hardcore heist yet, start there. This guide covers the long-term progression arc.

Phase 1: The First Heist (Rank Apprentice)

Your first Hardcore heist is the most dangerous because you have zero tools beyond the crowbar and zero skill leaflets.

What to Do

  • Accept the lowest-risk house from the job board
  • Enter through a window (quieter than doors with crowbar)
  • Grab only 3-4 visible items
  • Exit within 60 seconds
  • Sell everything immediately, even low-value items

What Not to Do

  • Do not attempt to open locked doors without a lockpick
  • Do not stay in a house for more than 2 minutes
  • Do not carry more than you can afford to lose

Goal of Phase 1: Accumulate about $500-$1,000 for a basic lockpick set.

item.title: Crime Simulator Loot Selling Strategy: Maximize Every Heist
item.description:
The fastest way from zero to quota: stack item requests, sell duplicate skill leaflets before basic loot, and never fence high-value items at the same fence twice in one day (prices drop).

The Common Mistake

Most new players sell every item they find to the nearest fence immediately. This is leaving money on the table. Crime Simulator's economy has hidden mechanics that punish haphazard selling and reward patient strategy.

Rule 1: Item Requests First

The item request system offers 50-100% premium over fence prices. Before selling anything:

item.title: Crime Simulator Tool Upgrades and Advanced Tools
item.description:
The Master Key from Update 1.3 is the best late-game tool investment. It opens most standard locks instantly, dramatically speeding up heists.

Upgrade Philosophy

Basic tools get the job done, but upgraded versions save time, reduce noise, and fail less often. Tool upgrades are not linear -- some require skill leaflets, others need specific rank milestones.

Every upgrade reduces the risk of a broken tool ending your heist prematurely.

Tool Quality Tiers

Tier Example Break Rate Cost to Repair Unlock
Basic Basic Lockpick High ~$100-200 Starting shop
Standard Standard Lockpick Medium ~$200-350 Rank 2-3
Professional Pro Lockpick Set Low ~$400-600 Rank 4-5
Premium Master Key (1.3) Very Low ~$800+ 1.3 content

Higher tiers unlock larger sweet spots on lockpicking mini-games and reduce the noise generated.

item.title: How to Make Triple Dollar Strain: Best Custom Mix Recipes
item.description:
Short answer: Triple Dollar strain = OG Kush + Banana + Battery + Cuke, mixed in that order at a Mixing Station. Sells for $120+ per unit at Peddler rank. Total ingredient cost: ~$10. This is the most searched custom strain recipe on Reddit and TikTok because it offers the best profit-per-effort ratio among all 4-ingredient mixes.

Triple Dollar Recipe Breakdown

Ingredient Source Cost Effect on Strain
OG Kush (base) Albert at motel ~$3/grow Base strain type
Banana Gas station or Albert ~$2 Adds yield boost
Battery Dan's Hardware ~$3 Adds potency multiplier
Cuke Gas station or Albert ~$2 Adds stability
Total ~$10 Sell price: $120+

Popular Custom Strains

Here are community-discovered custom strain recipes that sell for higher than standard mixes:

item.title: All Properties Ranked: Best Buy Order from Starter to Manor
item.description:
Short answer: Buy order: Bungalow ($6K at Hustler) to Barn ($25K at Dealer) to Docks Warehouse ($50K at Hoodlum V) to Hyland Manor ($250K after completing Finishing the Job quest). Do NOT buy the Manor until Kingpin rank - the $250K is better spent on automation equipment. The Barn is the best value property in the game.

All Properties Comparison Table

Property Cost Employee Cap Loading Bays Best For Buy At Rank
Burned RV Free (starter) 0 0 Tutorial only Start
Apartment Free (given) 0 0 First mixing station Start
Bungalow $6,000 5 1 Mid-game mixing Hustler
The Barn $25,000 10 2 Full automation Dealer
Docks Warehouse $50,000 12 2 Endgame production Hoodlum V
Hyland Manor $250,000 16 3 Max scale Kingpin

Bungalow ($6,000) - The First Real Upgrade

Buy this as soon as you have $6K. Located at Ray's Realty (northeast of the burned RV marker). The Bungalow has space for 4 Mixing Stations, 2 shelves, and 5 employees.

item.title: Bungalow Setup Guide: First Property Optimization
item.description:
Short answer: The Bungalow ($6,000, 5 employee slots, 1 loading bay) is your first real property. Optimal setup: 2 Mixing Stations on the south wall, 2 shelves on the north wall, 1 Botanist in the middle, 2 Handlers on route duty, 1 Packer at the loading bay. This fits the OG Kush 4-mix production line with room to grow into Meth when you unlock Dealer rank.

Bungalow Layout Blueprint

The Bungalow has a single open floor plan. Unlike the Barn, there are no stairs or separate rooms to worry about. You have roughly a 8x6 tile area to work with.

item.title: Mixing Station Automation: Handler + Chemist + Packer Setup
item.description:
Short answer: Three employees automate mixing: the Chemist mixes ingredients at Mixing Stations, the Handler moves products between stations and shelves, and the Packer bags finished goods. Set up ingredient shelves feeding into daisy-chained Mixing Stations with the Handler on route, and your production runs AFK. Full auto requires 6+ Mixing Stations per recipe, 4+ shelves, and 2 Handlers minimum.

The Three Automation Roles

Schedule 1 has three employee types that form your automation pipeline. Each role handles a specific part of the production chain.

item.title: All 4 Suppliers Unlock Guide: Ingredients and Dead Drop Locations
item.description:
Short answer: There are 4 named suppliers in Schedule 1: Albert Hoover (weed seeds), Shirley Watts (pseudo for meth), Fungal Phil (shroom supplies), and Salvador Moreno (coca seeds). Each is unlocked by reaching Friendly status with one of their connections, then using Dead Drop orders to build relationship. Dan's Hardware is a shop (not a supplier NPC) -- the actual NPC shopkeeper is Dan Samwell.

All 4 Suppliers Reference

Portrait Supplier Region Products Sold Unlock Connection
Albert Hoover Northtown Marijuana Seeds (all tiers) Available from start
Shirley Watts Westville Pseudo (meth precursor) Elizabeth Homley or Kevin Oakley
Fungal Phil Downtown Shroom spores and supplies Elizabeth Homley or Kevin Oakley
Salvador Moreno Docks Coca Plant Seeds Higher rank connections

How to Unlock Each Supplier

Suppliers are unlocked by reaching Friendly status with one of their connections. You cannot unlock them just by finding their stash location.

item.title: Barn and Warehouse Setup: Best AFK Production Lines Per Property
item.description:
Short answer: The Barn ($25K) supports 10 employees and is the best mid-game automation property. The Docks Warehouse ($50K) supports 12 employees and is the endgame automation hub. Each property needs a different layout: Barn uses a 2-floor vertical chain, Warehouse uses a horizontal U-shape. Cocaine 8-mix at the Warehouse yields $305 net per unit with full automation.

Barn Setup (10 Employees)

The Barn is the first property where full automation becomes viable. Buy it from Ray's Realty for $25,000 once you reach Dealer rank.

item.title: All 6 Dealers: Best Customer Assignments for Max Profit
item.description:
Short answer: There are 6 dealers in Schedule 1: Benji Coleman, Molly Presley, Brad Crosby, Jane Lucero, Wei Long, and Leo Rivers. Each requires a buy-in ($500-$5,000) and unlocks at specific rank thresholds. Assign 8-10 customers per dealer. Prioritize low-spending customers for dealers -- keep your high-spending customers for direct sales. Since v0.4.3, dealers can serve 10 customers each.

All 6 Dealers Reference

Portrait Dealer Location Buy-In Cut Unlock Rank Max Customers
Benji Coleman Northtown, Motel Room #2 $500 20% Tutorial 10 (8 pre-v0.4.3)
Molly Presley Westville, Brown Apartment $1,000 20% Hoodlum I 10 (8)
Brad Crosby Downtown, Parking Garage (under overpass) $2,000 20% Hustler I 10 (8)
Jane Lucero Docks, Docks RV $3,000 20% Enforcer I 10 (8)
Wei Long Suburbia, Shack behind green house $4,000 20% Block Boss I 10 (8)
Leo Rivers Near Church $5,000 20% Kingpin I 10 (8)

How to Unlock Each Dealer

Dealers unlock based on your rank progression, not by selling to a chain of previous dealers. Each dealer appears when you reach the required rank.

item.title: Heat Levels Explained: What Triggers Police and How to Reduce It
item.description:
Short answer: Your Heat Level rises when you sell drugs in public, get spotted by police while carrying contraband, or commit violent acts. Heat has 4 levels: Investigating (minor suspicion), Wanted (active chase), Wanted Dead or Alive (lethal force), and Maximum (city-wide lockdown). Heat decreases by staying out of sight -- hiding in a garbage bin for 30-60 seconds clears Wanted status completely.

What Drives Heat Level Up

Your Heat Level is not a hidden number. It is a visible Wanted status that escalates based on player actions. The game uses a line-of-sight detection system -- police have to SEE you committing a crime to raise the level.

item.title: v0.4.5 Anniversary Update: Full Patch Notes and Meta Impact
item.description:
Short answer: v0.4.5 (March 30, 2026) added the Golden M1911 pistol, Big Sprinkler for faster plant watering, a reorder feature for the delivery app, and dynamic reticle for ranged weapons. Meta impact: Big Sprinkler changes Botanist efficiency and watering throughput. No major recipe or police behavior changes.

New Additions in v0.4.5

Feature Type Unlock Impact
Golden M1911 Weapon Casino vending machine (rare drop) Cosmetic, same stats as M1911
Big Sprinkler Equipment Dan's Hardware, $200 Waters 4 plants at once -- huge automation upgrade
Reorder Feature UI Automatic One-click repeat of last delivery order
Dynamic Reticle UI Automatic Crosshair changes size based on accuracy
Bug reporting tool System Steam overlay Report issues in-game

Big Sprinkler: The Real Game-Changer

The Big Sprinkler is the most impactful addition in v0.4.5. It replaces the basic sprinkler and covers a 4-plant area in one activation.

item.title: How to Escape Cops: Garbage Bin Hiding + Line of Sight Break
item.description:
Short answer: Run behind a building to break line of sight, then hide in a garbage bin for 30-60 seconds. That clears any Wanted level. If no bin is nearby, enter a safe house or use an Energy Drink speed boost to outrun the pursuing officers. Police pathfinding is weak -- they struggle with jumping over fences and navigating through buildings.

Escape Method Comparison

Not all escape methods are equal. Here is how the five reliable methods compare:

item.title: Schedule 1 Complete Update History (v0.1 to v0.4.5)
item.description:
Short answer: Schedule 1 launched March 24, 2025. The first major content update (v0.3.4) shipped two weeks later on April 7. v0.2.4 added co-op multiplayer. v0.4.5 Anniversary Update (March 30, 2026) added Big Sprinkler and Golden M1911. The game receives patches approximately every 4-6 weeks.

Full Version Timeline (source: Fandom Wiki)

Version Date Key Additions
v0.1.x Mar 24, 2025 Initial early access release, basic mixing, 1 zone
v0.1.14 Mar-Apr 2025 Multi-dragging, physics improvements
v0.1.15 Mar-Apr 2025 Pickpocketing, counter-offer system
v0.1.16 Mar-Apr 2025 Snake minigame, bug fixes
v0.2.4 Apr 2025 Co-op multiplayer (up to 4 players)
v0.2.5 Apr 2025 Customer behavior improvements
v0.2.6 Apr 2025 Packaging Station MK II
v0.2.7 Apr-May 2025 More effects, mixing changes
v0.2.8 Apr-May 2025 Mixing Station MK II
v0.2.9 Apr-May 2025 New ambient music, optimization, map additions
v0.3.4 2025.04.07 Pawn Shop, Fancy Stuff, functional shops
v0.3.5 2025.05.08 Jukebox, Storage Unit
v0.3.6 2025.06.08 Filters, Employee Tweaks, Save System improvements
v0.4.0 2025.08.28 Cartel Update -- Benzies Family, major content
v0.4.1 2025.11.02 Halloween Update
v0.4.2 2025.12.26 Shrooms Update
v0.4.3 2026.02.03 Storage Closets update
v0.4.4 2026.03.19 Weather Update
v0.4.5 2026.03.30 Anniversary Update -- Big Sprinkler, Golden M1911

What Each Major Version Changed

v0.1.x (Launch, Mar 2025): Core drug mixing and selling loop. Single zone map, basic suppliers, no employees. The game was functional but shallow.

item.title: Casino Blackjack and Ride the Bus: Fastest Money Multiplier
item.description:
Short answer: Play "Ride the Bus" not Blackjack. Reddit community analysis shows Ride the Bus has +25% expected value per hand vs Blackjack's -0.5%. The optimal strategy: save at your property before going, gamble, save after a big win, reload if you lose big. With $1,000 starting cash and optimal play, you can turn it into $20K+ in 30 minutes.

Casino Games Compared

The casino at the north end of the map has three gambling games. Here is how they stack up:

item.title: Mixing Mania $60 Quest: Step-by-Step Completion
item.description:
Short answer: The Mixing Mania quest asks you to create a strain worth $60 using a Mixing Station from Dan's Hardware. The recipe: OG Kush + Cuke + Banana + Donut = $68 strain. Buy OG Kush seeds from Albert, Cuke and Banana from gas station or Albert, Donut from the convenience store. Mix in this exact order at your Mixing Station.

Mixing Mania Quest Step by Step

1
Buy a Mixing Station $100

Go to Dan's Hardware (downtown) and buy a Mixing Station for $100. Place it in your apartment or Bungalow. You also need a Mixing Station Clipboard ($25) to configure the recipe.

item.title: $10K in First Hour: Delivery and Trash Dumping Route
item.description:
Short answer: Deliveries and trash dumping are the safest early-game methods. A single optimized route: take a delivery order ($50-100 per delivery), grab trash on the way ($5-15 per dump), and smash vending machines ($20-50 each). Repeat for 1 hour = $8K-$12K with zero police risk. This is the fastest legal-ish money before unlocking dealers.

Early Game Income Methods

Method Income Per Action Risk Time per Action Best For
Deliver orders $50-$150 None 2-4 minutes Steady income
Trash dumping $5-$15 per bag None 30 seconds Filler income
Smash vending machines $20-$50 Low (public) 10 seconds Quick cash
Sell to customers (street) $100-$400 Medium (police) 1-2 minutes Fast money
Casino gambling Variable None (with save) 5-30 minutes Big multiplier

The Optimal First-Hour Route

Setup: You need the Burned RV area as your base. Buy a trash can ($25 from Furniture shop) and place it at the RV. Grab a delivery order from your phone.

item.title: Strain Mix Calculator: Ingredient Chain Optimization
item.description:
Short answer: Use the Schedule 1 Calculator (schedule-1-calculator.com) for real-time ingredient chain analysis. The optimal mixing strategy: add ingredients in ascending value order. Each ingredient contributes different stat multipliers -- Battery adds potency, Cuke adds stability, Donut adds random effects. A 4-ingredient mix is the sweet spot for profit per station.

Ingredient Effect Reference

Each ingredient in Schedule 1 adds specific properties to the final strain. Understanding these effects helps you design better custom mixes.

item.title: Map Zones and Key Locations: Complete Area Guide
item.description:
Short answer: The Schedule 1 map has 5 main zones: Downtown, Motel Area, Industrial Zone, Docks, and Highway (Casino). Each zone has different suppliers, police density, and available properties. Downtown and Motel are safe for early game. Industrial and Docks are mid-to-late game zones. The Highway zone has the casino and is most active at night.

Zone-by-Zone Breakdown

Zone Police Density Properties Suppliers Best Time Risk Level
Downtown High (2 patrols + 1 station) Apartment (free) Dan Samwell (shop) Day Medium
Motel/Westville Low (1 patrol) Bungalow ($6K) Albert Hoover Any Low
Industrial Medium (1 patrol) Barn ($25K) Shirley Watts, Fungal Phil Day Medium
Docks Medium (1 night patrol) Docks Warehouse ($50K) Salvador Moreno Night (6PM-6AM) Medium
Highway/North Low (1 patrol) None Casino, vending machines Night Low

Downtown Zone

The starting area. Contains:
